└─────────────────────┘\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n## FastStream: The Broker Adapter\\\\n\\\\n[FastStream](https://faststream.ag2.ai/) is the **Pythonic adapter** that wraps low-level Kafka/RabbitMQ clients (like &#96;confluent-kafka&#96;, &#96;aio-pika&#96;) and provides a decorator-based, async/await, type-safe API.\\\\n\\\\n**Think of it like SQLAlchemy for message brokers** - it hides 200+ lines of boilerplate:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\n# WITHOUT FastStream (raw Kafka client)\\\\nfrom confluent_kafka import Consumer\\\\nconsumer = Consumer({'bootstrap.servers': 'localhost:9092', ...})\\\\nconsumer.subscribe(['user.created'])\\\\nwhile True:\\\\n    msg = consumer.poll(1.0)\\\\n    if msg:\\\\n        event_dict = json.loads(msg.value().decode())\\\\n        event = UserCreatedEvent(**event_dict)  # Manual conversion\\\\n        await handle_user_created(event)\\\\n        consumer.commit(msg)\\\\n\\\\n# WITH FastStream (what you actually write)\\\\nfrom faststream.confluent import KafkaBroker\\\\n\\\\nbroker = KafkaBroker(\\\\\\\"localhost:9092\\\\\\\")\\\\n\\\\n@broker.subscriber(\\\\\\\"user.created\\\\\\\")\\\\nasync def handle_user_created(event: UserCreatedEvent):  # ✅ Already a Pydantic model!\\\\n    # FastStream automatically:\\\\n    # - Polls Kafka, decodes bytes → JSON → Pydantic\\\\n    # - Runs middleware (telemetry, circuit breaker, rate limiter)\\\\n    # - Commits offset on success, sends to DLQ on failure\\\\n    print(f\\\\\\\"User {event.user_id} created!\\\\\\\")\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**What FastStream provides:**\\\\n\\\\n- 🎯 **Decorator-based subscriptions**: &#96;@broker.subscriber(\\\\\\\"topic\\\\\\\")&#96;\\\\n- 🔄 **Automatic Pydantic conversion**: Type hints → auto-deserialization\\\\n- 🔌 **Middleware hooks**: Circuit breakers, rate limiting, telemetry, metrics\\\\n- 🏥 **Native health checks**: &#96;make_ping_asgi&#96; endpoint for broker status\\\\n- 🔀 **Unified API**: Same code works for Kafka, RabbitMQ, NATS, Redis Streams\\\\n\\\\n&#96;python-eventing&#96; uses FastStream for all Kafka/RabbitMQ interactions, giving you a clean, Pythonic API while handling all the low-level complexity.\\\\n\\\\n## Setup\\\\n\\\\n### Database schema\\\\n\\\\nThe outbox table stores events transactionally with your business data:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql\\\\nCREATE TABLE outbox_events (\\\\n    event_id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,\\\\n    event_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\\\n    aggregate_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\\\n    payload JSONB NOT NULL,\\\\n    occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\\\\n    published BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,\\\\n    failed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,\\\\n    attempt_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL,\\\\n    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\\\\n    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\n    failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\\\\n    error_message TEXT\\\\n);\\\\n\\\\nCREATE INDEX idx_outbox_unpublished ON outbox_events (published, created_at);\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Application startup\\\\n\\\\nInitialize the outbox repository and event bus at application startup:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\\\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine\\\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\\\nfrom messaging.core import build_event_bus\\\\n\\\\napp = FastAPI()\\\\n\\\\n@app.on_event(\\\\\\\"startup\\\\\\\")\\\\nasync def startup():\\\\n    # Database engine\\\\n    engine = create_async_engine(\\\\\\\"postgresql+asyncpg://...\\\\\\\")\\\\n    \\\\n    # Outbox repository\\\\n    outbox_repo = SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository(engine)\\\\n    app.state.outbox_repository = outbox_repo\\\\n    \\\\n    # Optional: EventBus for advanced patterns\\\\n    event_bus = build_event_bus()\\\\n    app.state.event_bus = event_bus\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**CDC Publishing:** Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC automatically detects outbox table changes and publishes to Kafka. See [&#96;debezium-cdc-architecture.md&#96;](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/debezium-cdc-architecture.html) for configuration.\\\\n\\\\n## Quick Start: Transactional Outbox\\\\n\\\\nThe **core pattern** is the transactional outbox - persist events atomically with your business data:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\nfrom fastapi import Depends\\\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\\\n\\\\n# Define domain event\\\\nclass UserCreated(BaseEvent):\\\\n    event_type: str = \\\\\\\"user.created\\\\\\\"\\\\n    aggregate_id: str\\\\n    user_id: int\\\\n    email: str\\\\n\\\\n# Simple, direct approach (recommended)\\\\n@app.post(\\\\\\\"/users\\\\\\\")\\\\nasync def create_user(\\\\n    data: CreateUserRequest,\\\\n    session = Depends(get_session),\\\\n    outbox_repo: SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository = Depends(get_outbox_repo)\\\\n):\\\\n    # 1. Business logic\\\\n    user = User(**data.dict())\\\\n    session.add(user)\\\\n\\\\n    # 2. Persist event to outbox (same transaction)\\\\n    await outbox_repo.add_event(\\\\n        UserCreated(\\\\n            aggregate_id=f\\\\\\\"user-{user.id}\\\\\\\",\\\\n            user_id=user.id,\\\\n            email=user.email,\\\\n        ),\\\\n        session=session\\\\n    )\\\\n\\\\n    # 3. Commit both atomically\\\\n    await session.commit()\\\\n\\\\n    # 4. Kafka Connect (Debezium CDC) detects the outbox insert and publishes to Kafka\\\\n    return {\\\\\\\"user_id\\\\\\\": user.id}\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**Result**: Guaranteed delivery, no lost events, atomic writes.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Advanced: EventBus (Optional)\\\\n\\\\nFor **decoupled architectures** with multiple side effects per event, use the **EventBus** abstraction layer:\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import OutboxEventHandler\\\\n\\\\n# Access EventBus (initialized at startup)\\\\nevent_bus = request.app.state.event_bus\\\\noutbox_repo = request.app.state.outbox_repository\\\\n\\\\n# Register handler (typically at startup)\\\\nevent_bus.register(UserCreated, OutboxEventHandler(outbox_repo))\\\\n\\\\n# Dispatch (same result as direct add_event, but decoupled)\\\\nawait event_bus.dispatch(UserCreated(...))\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n**When to use EventBus**:\\\\n\\\\n- ✅ Multiple side effects per event (audit, metrics, cache)\\\\n- ✅ Need lifecycle hooks for observability\\\\n- ✅ Testing isolation (enable/disable toggle)\\\\n- ✅ Decorator-based handler registration\\\\n\\\\n**When NOT needed**:\\\\n\\\\n- ❌ Simple event persistence (use direct &#96;outbox_repo.add_event()&#96;)\\\\n- ❌ Single handler per event\\\\n- ❌ No need for hooks/tracing\\\\n\\\\n📚 **[EventBus Documentation](./docs/eventbus/usage-guide.md)** - Complete guide for advanced patterns\\\\n\\\\n## Distribution\\\\n\\\\n- PyPI distribution name: &#96;python-eventing&#96;\\\\n- Python import package: &#96;messaging&#96;\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\\\n# Install\\\\npip install python-eventing\\\\n\\\\n# Import\\\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\nServices should consume the published package rather than source checkout. Kafka remains shared infrastructure with local producer/consumer clients per service.\\\\n\\\\n## Local development\\\\n\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;powershell\\\\npoetry install\\\\npoetry build\\\\npoetry run pytest\\\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\\\n\\\\n### Testing\\\\n\\\\n**Local (Windows):**\\\\n- Run non-RabbitMQ tests: &#96;poetry run pytest tests/ -v -m \\\\\\\"not requires_rabbitmq\\\\\\\"&#96;\\\\n- RabbitMQ integration tests fail due to Docker Desktop networking limitations\\\\n\\\\n**CI/CD (Recommended for complete coverage):**\\\\n- GitHub Actions runs all 192 tests on Linux (100% pass rate)\\\\n- Workflows configured in &#96;.github/workflows/&#96;\\\\n- See &#96;.github/workflows/README.md&#96; for details\\\\n\\\\n**For complete local test coverage on Windows:** See [Windows Testing with WSL2](docs/WINDOWS_TESTING_WITH_WSL2.md) (requires Docker Desktop WSL integration setup).\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\"}],\"structuredContent\":{\"ok\":true,\"path\":\"c:\\\\coding\\\\gridflow-microservices-codex-taskmaster\\\\microservices\\\\eventing\\\\README.md\",\"content\":\"# Eventing\\n\\n[Documentation Status](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)\\n[Tests](https://github.com/firstunicorn/python-eventing/actions)\\n[Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/)\\n[License](LICENSE)\\n[Code Style](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)\\n[Validate Dependencies](https://github.com/firstunicorn/python-eventing/actions/workflows/validate-dependencies.yml)\\n\\n## Table of contents\\n\\n- [Installation](#installation)\\n- [When to use this package](#when-to-use-this-package)\\n- [Comparison with alternatives](#comparison-with-alternatives)\\n- [Scope](#scope)\\n- [Quick start: transactional outbox](#quick-start-transactional-outbox)\\n- [Setup](#setup)\\n- [Advanced: EventBus](#advanced-eventbus-optional)\\n- [Documentation](#documentation)\\n- [Local development](#local-development)\\n\\nPackage-first universal event infrastructure for microservices.\\n\\n📚 **[Full Documentation](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)** - Comprehensive guides and API reference\\n\\n## Installation\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\\npip install python-eventing\\n# or\\npoetry add python-eventing\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**Import package name:** &#96;messaging&#96; (distribution is &#96;python-eventing&#96;)\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**Requirements:**\\n- Python 3.12+\\n- PostgreSQL (outbox persistence)\\n- Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC (publishing infrastructure)\\n\\n## When to use this package\\n\\n**Use &#96;python-eventing&#96; if you need:**\\n- Guaranteed event delivery via transactional outbox pattern\\n- Kafka-based microservice messaging with CDC publishing\\n- Dead letter queue handling with database bookkeeping\\n- Idempotent consumer patterns with durable deduplication\\n- Native broker integration (FastStream, Debezium CDC, RabbitMQ DLX)\\n\\n**Consider alternatives if:**\\n- Simple in-process events only → [&#96;pyventus&#96;](https://github.com/mdapena/pyventus)\\n- FastAPI request-scoped events → [&#96;fastapi-events&#96;](https://github.com/melvinkcx/fastapi-events)\\n- Non-Kafka message brokers without CDC support\\n- No need for durable outbox persistence\\n\\nSupport scale: &#96;❌&#96; none, &#96;✅&#96; basic, &#96;✅✅&#96; strong, &#96;✅✅✅&#96; first-class\\n\\n## Comparison with alternatives\\n\\n&#96;python-eventing&#96; prioritizes **durable messaging** (transactional outbox + CDC) and **Kafka/RabbitMQ integration** over in-process event simplicity:\\n\\n| Capability | &#96;python-eventing&#96; | [&#96;pyventus&#96;](https://github.com/mdapena/pyventus) | [&#96;fastapi-events&#96;](https://github.com/melvinkcx/fastapi-events) | Notes |\\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\\n| Transactional outbox | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Durable local DB plus outbox boundary is a core feature here |\\n| Kafka data plane | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | This package is built for Kafka-backed microservice messaging |\\n| DLQ handling | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Leverages native RabbitMQ DLX and Kafka Connect DLQ SMT with database bookkeeping |\\n| Health checks for eventing runtime | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Outbox health checks plus FastStream ASGI broker health endpoint |\\n| Typed cross-service event contracts | ✅✅ | ✅ | ✅✅ | &#96;python-eventing&#96; and &#96;fastapi-events&#96; are stronger on explicit payload modeling |\\n| Decorator subscriber registration | ✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | ✅✅ | &#96;EventBus.subscriber(...)&#96; exists now; &#96;pyventus&#96; is still the most polished here |\\n| In-process dispatch backend abstraction | ✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | ✅ | &#96;DispatchBackend&#96; exists here; &#96;pyventus&#96; offers a broader processor model |\\n| Lifecycle hooks / callbacks | ✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | ✅ | &#96;DispatchHooks&#96; covers dispatch, success, failure, disabled, and debug |\\n| Debug / disable controls | ✅✅ | ✅✅ | ✅✅✅ | &#96;DispatchSettings(enabled, debug)&#96; is implemented; &#96;fastapi-events&#96; is strongest for app-level toggling |\\n| Observability / telemetry polish | ✅✅ | ✅ | ✅✅✅ | FastStream native middlewares (KafkaTelemetryMiddleware, KafkaPrometheusMiddleware) integrated |\\n| Resilience middleware | ✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | CircuitBreakerMiddleware (prevents cascading failures) and RateLimiterMiddleware (throttles consumption rate) |\\n| CDC-based outbox publishing | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC handles outbox-to-Kafka publishing |\\n| Consumer dedup helper | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | &#96;IdempotentConsumerBase&#96; now uses a durable processed-message store instead of process memory |\\n| Durable cross-service idempotency | ✅✅✅ | ❌ | ❌ | &#96;IProcessedMessageStore&#96; plus &#96;SqlAlchemyProcessedMessageStore&#96; provide transactional duplicate protection |\\n| Consumer batch handling | ❌ | ❌ | ✅✅✅ | &#96;fastapi-events&#96; supports &#96;handle_many(...)&#96;; this package stays one-message-per-consume today |\\n| FastAPI-local event flow | ❌ | ✅ | ✅✅✅ | This package intentionally avoids request-lifecycle middleware eventing |\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**Included:**\\n- Transactional outbox primitives (write-side only; CDC handles publishing)\\n- Event contracts and registry\\n- Kafka/RabbitMQ consumer base classes with idempotency\\n- Native broker integration (Kafka Connect CDC, RabbitMQ DLX, FastStream middlewares)\\n- In-process emitter/subscriber facade and hooks\\n- DLQ bookkeeping consumer for database flag synchronization\\n\\n**NOT included (delegated to external systems):**\\n- Event publishing (handled by Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC)\\n- Message broker infrastructure setup (use official Kafka/RabbitMQ documentation)\\n- Schema registry management (use Confluent Schema Registry or alternatives)\\n- Request-scoped FastAPI event middleware (intentionally avoided)\\n- Consumer batch handling (use &#96;fastapi-events&#96; if needed)\\n\\n## Documentation\\n\\n📖 **[Integration Guide](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/integration-guide.html)** - Step-by-step integration instructions\\n\\n🔍 **[API Reference](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/autoapi/index.html)** - Complete API documentation\\n\\n📋 **[Event Catalog](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/event-catalog.html)** - Available event types and contracts\\n\\n### Key topics\\n\\n- [Transactional Outbox Pattern](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/transactional-outbox.html) - Guaranteed event delivery (PRIMARY)\\n- [Cross-Service Communication](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cross-service-communication.html) - Database isolation, Kafka/RabbitMQ architecture, production deployment\\n- [Idempotent Consumers](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/consumer-transactions.html) - Duplicate message handling\\n- [Health Checks](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/autoapi/eventing/infrastructure/health/index.html) - Monitoring outbox and broker status\\n\\n**Architecture note**: This package handles the **write side** of the outbox pattern (persisting events transactionally with business data). **Publishing** is delegated to Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC, which captures outbox table changes and publishes to Kafka. The **bridge component** (part of standard architecture) forwards events from Kafka to RabbitMQ for services preferring AMQP. Dead letter handling leverages native broker mechanisms (RabbitMQ DLX, Kafka Connect DLQ SMT) with a minimal bookkeeping consumer to maintain database failed-event flags.\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n### Quick Overview\\n\\n**Cross-service communication pattern:** Each microservice has its own PostgreSQL database (database-per-service). Services communicate via **Kafka** (shared event backbone) and **RabbitMQ** (dual-broker pattern via bridge).\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\\nflowchart LR\\n    subgraph Service_A[\\\"Service A (Producer)\\\"]\\n        AppA[FastAPI Route]\\n        DB1[(PostgreSQL A\u003cbr/\u003eoutbox_events)]\\n    end\\n\\n    subgraph Infrastructure[\\\"Shared Infrastructure\\\"]\\n        CDC[Kafka Connect CDC]\\n        Kafka([Apache Kafka\u003cbr/\u003eTopic: events])\\n        Bridge[Eventing Bridge\u003cbr/\u003eKafka → RabbitMQ]\\n        RMQ([RabbitMQ\u003cbr/\u003eExchange: events])\\n    end\\n\\n    subgraph Service_B[\\\"Service B (Kafka Consumer)\\\"]\\n        Handler1[Event Handler]\\n        DB2[(PostgreSQL B\u003cbr/\u003eprocessed_messages)]\\n    end\\n\\n    subgraph Service_C[\\\"Service C (RabbitMQ Consumer)\\\"]\\n        Handler2[Event Handler]\\n        DB3[(PostgreSQL C\u003cbr/\u003eprocessed_messages)]\\n    end\\n\\n    DLQ([DLQ: Native Broker])\\n\\n    %% Primary Path (Kafka)\\n    AppA --\u003e|1. Atomic Write| DB1\\n    DB1 -.-\u003e|2. CDC Monitor| CDC\\n    CDC --\u003e|3. Publish| Kafka\\n    Kafka --\u003e|4. Subscribe| Handler1\\n    Handler1 --\u003e|5. Dedup Check| DB2\\n\\n    %% Bridge Path (RabbitMQ)\\n    Kafka -.-\u003e|6. Bridge Forward| Bridge\\n    Bridge --\u003e|7. Publish| RMQ\\n    RMQ -.-\u003e|8. Subscribe| Handler2\\n    Handler2 --\u003e|9. Dedup Check| DB3\\n\\n    %% Error Flow\\n    Handler1 -.-\u003e|Exception| DLQ\\n    Handler2 -.-\u003e|Exception| DLQ\\n\\n    %% Styling\\n    classDef app fill:#4CAF50,stroke:#2E7D32,color:#fff\\n    classDef db fill:#2196F3,stroke:#1565C0,color:#fff\\n    classDef broker fill:#FF9800,stroke:#E65100,color:#fff\\n    classDef infra fill:#9C27B0,stroke:#6A1B9A,color:#fff\\n    classDef error fill:#F44336,stroke:#C62828,color:#fff\\n\\n    class AppA,Handler1,Handler2 app\\n    class DB1,DB2,DB3 db\\n    class Kafka,RMQ broker\\n    class CDC,Bridge infra\\n    class DLQ error\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**Key points:**\\n\\n- Each service has **separate PostgreSQL database** (DB1, DB2, DB3)\\n- **Kafka** is the shared event bus connecting all services\\n- **Kafka Connect CDC** watches Service A's outbox and publishes to Kafka\\n- **Service B** consumes directly from Kafka\\n- **Bridge** forwards Kafka → RabbitMQ for services preferring AMQP\\n- **Service C** consumes from RabbitMQ\\n- Both Kafka and RabbitMQ are part of the **standard architecture**\\n\\n**Guarantees:**\\n\\n- **Write Phase**: ✅ Atomic (business data + event in same transaction, same database)\\n- **Publish Phase**: ✅ At-least-once (CDC retries on failure)\\n- **Consume Phase**: ✅ Exactly-once (idempotency via processed message store in consumer's database)\\n\\n📖 **[Cross-Service Communication Guide](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cross-service-communication.html)** - Detailed explanation with production deployment patterns\\n\\n### Database isolation and event flow\\n\\n**Critical architecture principle:** Each service maintains its **own PostgreSQL database** (database-per-service pattern). Services do NOT directly access each other's databases. **Kafka acts as the shared event bus** connecting isolated services.\\n\\n**Complete event flow:**\\n\\n1. **Service A (Producer)** writes event to its own &#96;outbox_events&#96; table (in postgres-a)\\n2. **Kafka Connect CDC** monitors Service A's outbox table via PostgreSQL WAL\\n3. **CDC publishes** event to **Kafka topic** (shared infrastructure)\\n4. **Service B (Consumer)** subscribes to **Kafka topic**, processes event, stores idempotency check in its own &#96;processed_messages&#96; table (in postgres-b)\\n5. **Bridge Service** forwards events from **Kafka → RabbitMQ** (part of standard architecture)\\n6. **Service C (Consumer)** subscribes to **RabbitMQ exchange**, processes event, stores idempotency check in its own &#96;processed_messages&#96; table (in postgres-c)\\n\\n**Result:** Services remain isolated (no shared database), communicate via Kafka, and maintain exactly-once processing guarantees via their own idempotency stores.\\n\\n### Full Component Architecture\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n│                         YOUR SERVICE (FastAPI)                               │\\n│                                                                              │\\n│  ┌──────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │\\n│  │   Routes     │──┬──▶│      python-eventing (SHARED CONTRACTS)       │    │\\n│  └──────────────┘  │   │                                               │    │\\n│                    │   │  • BaseEvent (Pydantic base)                  │    │\\n│                    │   │  • IOutboxRepository (Protocol)               │    │\\n│                    │   │  • IProcessedMessageStore (Protocol)          │    │\\n│                    │   └───────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘    │\\n│                    │                       │ Uses contracts                 │\\n│                    │                       ▼                                │\\n│                    │   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │\\n│                    │   │         python-eventing (implementations)     │    │\\n│                    │   │                                               │    │\\n│                    │   │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐  │    │\\n│                    │   │  │  WRITE SIDE (Primary)                  │  │    │\\n│                    └───┼──│  • SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository          │  │    │\\n│                        │  │    (implements IOutboxRepository)      │  │    │\\n│                        │  │  • OutboxEventHandler                  │  │    │\\n│                        │  │                                        │  │    │\\n│                        │  │  OPTIONAL (In-process dispatch)        │  │    │\\n│                        │  │  • EventBus (NOT wired to outbox)      │  │    │\\n│                        │  └────────────────────────────────────────┘  │    │\\n│                        └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │\\n└────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n                                     │ Writes to\\n                                     ▼\\n                          ┌─────────────────────┐\\n                          │   PostgreSQL DB     │\\n                          │                     │\\n                          │ ┌─────────────────┐ │\\n                          │ │ Business Tables │ │\\n                          │ └─────────────────┘ │\\n                          │ ┌─────────────────┐ │◀─┐\\n                          │ │ outbox_event_   │ │  │ Monitors\\n                          │ │ record          │ │  │ (WAL)\\n                          │ └────────┬────────┘ │  │\\n                          │ ┌────────▼────────┐ │  │\\n                          │ │ processed_      │ │  │\\n                          │ │ messages        │ │  │\\n                          │ └─────────────────┘ │  │\\n                          └─────────────────────┘  │\\n                                     │              │\\n                 ┌───────────────────┼──────────────┘\\n                 │                   │\\n                 │                   ▼\\n                 │    ┌──────────────────────────────────┐\\n                 │    │  EXTERNAL INFRASTRUCTURE         │\\n                 │    │  (Not provided by this package)  │\\n                 │    │                                  │\\n                 │    │  Kafka Connect + Debezium CDC    │\\n                 │    │  - PostgreSQL Connector          │\\n                 │    │  - Outbox Event Router SMT       │\\n                 │    └──────────────┬───────────────────┘\\n                 │                   │ Publishes\\n                 │                   ▼\\n                 │            ┌──────────────┐\\n                 │            │    Kafka     │\\n                 │            │   Topics     │\\n                 │            └──┬────────┬──┘\\n                 │               │        │\\n                 │               │        │\\n            Bookkeeping          │        │\\n            updates              │        │\\n                 │               │        │\\n                 │               ▼        ▼\\n                 │    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n                 │    │         FASTSTREAM BROKER LAYER                      │\\n                 │    │  (Broker abstraction + middleware)                   │\\n                 │    │                                                      │\\n                 │    │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │\\n                 │    │  │  KafkaBroker (from faststream.confluent)       │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • @broker.subscriber(\\\"topic\\\") decorators      │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • broker.publish() wrapper                    │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • Pydantic auto-deserialization               │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • make_ping_asgi health endpoint              │ │\\n                 │    │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │\\n                 │    │                                                      │\\n                 │    │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │\\n                 │    │  │  MIDDLEWARE STACK (Wired)                      │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • CircuitBreakerMiddleware (resilience)       │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • RateLimiterMiddleware (optional, disabled)  │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • KafkaPrometheusMiddleware (metrics)         │ │\\n                 │    │  │  • KafkaTelemetryMiddleware (OTel)             │ │\\n                 │    │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │\\n                 │    └──────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘\\n                 │                           │\\n                 │                           │ Consumes\\n                 │                           ▼\\n                 │    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\\n                 │    │  DOWNSTREAM SERVICES                                 │\\n                 │    │                                                      │\\n                 │    │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\\n                 │    │  │  READ SIDE (python-eventing)                 │   │\\n                 │    │  │                                              │   │\\n                 │    │  │  @broker.subscriber(\\\"user.created\\\")          │   │\\n                 │    │  │  async def handle(event: UserCreated):       │   │\\n                 │    │  │      # FastStream deserializes to Pydantic   │   │\\n                 │    │  │      ...                                     │   │\\n                 │    │  │                                              │   │\\n                 │    │  │  OR use IdempotentConsumerBase for:          │   │\\n                 │    │  │  • Programmatic idempotency control          │   │\\n                 │    │  │  • Dynamic/polymorphic event types           │   │\\n                 │    │  │  • Legacy dict-based handling                │   │\\n                 │    │  │                                              │   │\\n                 │    │  │  Components:                                 │   │\\n                │    │  │  • EventRegistry (event type→class mapping)  │   │\\n                │    │  │  • SqlAlchemyProcessedMessageStore           │   │\\n                │    │  │    (implements IProcessedMessageStore)       │   │\\n                │    │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\\n                │    │                                                      │\\n                │    │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\\n                │    │  │  BRIDGE COMPONENT (Standard Architecture)    │   │\\n                │    │  │  • BridgeConsumer (Kafka→RabbitMQ bridge)    │   │\\n                │    │  │    (manual idempotency, not base class)      │   │\\n                │    │  │  • Part of dual-broker event distribution    │   │\\n                │    │  │                                              │   │\\n                │    │  │  RabbitMQ Broker Middleware Stack:           │   │\\n                │    │  │  • CircuitBreakerMiddleware (resilience)     │   │\\n                │    │  │  • RateLimiterMiddleware (optional)          │   │\\n                │    │  │  • RabbitPrometheusMiddleware (metrics)      │   │\\n                │    │  │  • TelemetryMiddleware (OTel, shared)        │   │\\n                 │    │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\\n                 │    │                                  │                   │\\n                 │    │                                  │ On failure        │\\n                 │    │                                  ▼                   │\\n                 │    │                        ┌──────────────────┐          │\\n                 │    │                        │ EXTERNAL DLQ     │          │\\n                 │    │                        │ (broker native)  │          │\\n                 │    │                        │ • Kafka Connect  │          │\\n                 │    │                        │   DLQ SMT        │          │\\n                 │    │                        │ • RabbitMQ DLX   │          │\\n                 │    │                        └─────────┬────────┘          │\\n                 │    │                                  │                   │\\n                 │    │                                  ▼                   │\\n                 │    │                        ┌──────────────────┐          │\\n                 │    │                        │  DLQ Bookkeeper  │          │\\n                 │    │                        │  Consumer        │          │\\n                 │    │                        │  (@broker.sub)   │          │\\n                 │    │                        └──────────────────┘          │\\n                 │    │                                  │                   │\\n                 └────┼──────────────────────────────────┘                   │\\n                      │                                                      │\\n                      └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\\n                                                 │\\n                                                 ▼\\n                                    ┌─────────────────────┐\\n                                    │  Admin/Ops API      │\\n                                    │  • DLQAdminService  │\\n                                    │  • Replay API       │\\n                                    │  • Health Checks    │\\n                                    └─────────────────────┘\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n## FastStream: The Broker Adapter\\n\\n[FastStream](https://faststream.ag2.ai/) is the **Pythonic adapter** that wraps low-level Kafka/RabbitMQ clients (like &#96;confluent-kafka&#96;, &#96;aio-pika&#96;) and provides a decorator-based, async/await, type-safe API.\\n\\n**Think of it like SQLAlchemy for message brokers** - it hides 200+ lines of boilerplate:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\n# WITHOUT FastStream (raw Kafka client)\\nfrom confluent_kafka import Consumer\\nconsumer = Consumer({'bootstrap.servers': 'localhost:9092', ...})\\nconsumer.subscribe(['user.created'])\\nwhile True:\\n    msg = consumer.poll(1.0)\\n    if msg:\\n        event_dict = json.loads(msg.value().decode())\\n        event = UserCreatedEvent(**event_dict)  # Manual conversion\\n        await handle_user_created(event)\\n        consumer.commit(msg)\\n\\n# WITH FastStream (what you actually write)\\nfrom faststream.confluent import KafkaBroker\\n\\nbroker = KafkaBroker(\\\"localhost:9092\\\")\\n\\n@broker.subscriber(\\\"user.created\\\")\\nasync def handle_user_created(event: UserCreatedEvent):  # ✅ Already a Pydantic model!\\n    # FastStream automatically:\\n    # - Polls Kafka, decodes bytes → JSON → Pydantic\\n    # - Runs middleware (telemetry, circuit breaker, rate limiter)\\n    # - Commits offset on success, sends to DLQ on failure\\n    print(f\\\"User {event.user_id} created!\\\")\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**What FastStream provides:**\\n\\n- 🎯 **Decorator-based subscriptions**: &#96;@broker.subscriber(\\\"topic\\\")&#96;\\n- 🔄 **Automatic Pydantic conversion**: Type hints → auto-deserialization\\n- 🔌 **Middleware hooks**: Circuit breakers, rate limiting, telemetry, metrics\\n- 🏥 **Native health checks**: &#96;make_ping_asgi&#96; endpoint for broker status\\n- 🔀 **Unified API**: Same code works for Kafka, RabbitMQ, NATS, Redis Streams\\n\\n&#96;python-eventing&#96; uses FastStream for all Kafka/RabbitMQ interactions, giving you a clean, Pythonic API while handling all the low-level complexity.\\n\\n## Setup\\n\\n### Database schema\\n\\nThe outbox table stores events transactionally with your business data:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql\\nCREATE TABLE outbox_events (\\n    event_id VARCHAR(36) PRIMARY KEY,\\n    event_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\n    aggregate_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,\\n    payload JSONB NOT NULL,\\n    occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\\n    published BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,\\n    failed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL,\\n    attempt_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL,\\n    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,\\n    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\\n    failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,\\n    error_message TEXT\\n);\\n\\nCREATE INDEX idx_outbox_unpublished ON outbox_events (published, created_at);\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Application startup\\n\\nInitialize the outbox repository and event bus at application startup:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\nfrom messaging.core import build_event_bus\\n\\napp = FastAPI()\\n\\n@app.on_event(\\\"startup\\\")\\nasync def startup():\\n    # Database engine\\n    engine = create_async_engine(\\\"postgresql+asyncpg://...\\\")\\n    \\n    # Outbox repository\\n    outbox_repo = SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository(engine)\\n    app.state.outbox_repository = outbox_repo\\n    \\n    # Optional: EventBus for advanced patterns\\n    event_bus = build_event_bus()\\n    app.state.event_bus = event_bus\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**CDC Publishing:** Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC automatically detects outbox table changes and publishes to Kafka. See [&#96;debezium-cdc-architecture.md&#96;](https://python-eventing.readthedocs.io/en/latest/debezium-cdc-architecture.html) for configuration.\\n\\n## Quick Start: Transactional Outbox\\n\\nThe **core pattern** is the transactional outbox - persist events atomically with your business data:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nfrom fastapi import Depends\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\n\\n# Define domain event\\nclass UserCreated(BaseEvent):\\n    event_type: str = \\\"user.created\\\"\\n    aggregate_id: str\\n    user_id: int\\n    email: str\\n\\n# Simple, direct approach (recommended)\\n@app.post(\\\"/users\\\")\\nasync def create_user(\\n    data: CreateUserRequest,\\n    session = Depends(get_session),\\n    outbox_repo: SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository = Depends(get_outbox_repo)\\n):\\n    # 1. Business logic\\n    user = User(**data.dict())\\n    session.add(user)\\n\\n    # 2. Persist event to outbox (same transaction)\\n    await outbox_repo.add_event(\\n        UserCreated(\\n            aggregate_id=f\\\"user-{user.id}\\\",\\n            user_id=user.id,\\n            email=user.email,\\n        ),\\n        session=session\\n    )\\n\\n    # 3. Commit both atomically\\n    await session.commit()\\n\\n    # 4. Kafka Connect (Debezium CDC) detects the outbox insert and publishes to Kafka\\n    return {\\\"user_id\\\": user.id}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**Result**: Guaranteed delivery, no lost events, atomic writes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Advanced: EventBus (Optional)\\n\\nFor **decoupled architectures** with multiple side effects per event, use the **EventBus** abstraction layer:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import OutboxEventHandler\\n\\n# Access EventBus (initialized at startup)\\nevent_bus = request.app.state.event_bus\\noutbox_repo = request.app.state.outbox_repository\\n\\n# Register handler (typically at startup)\\nevent_bus.register(UserCreated, OutboxEventHandler(outbox_repo))\\n\\n# Dispatch (same result as direct add_event, but decoupled)\\nawait event_bus.dispatch(UserCreated(...))\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**When to use EventBus**:\\n\\n- ✅ Multiple side effects per event (audit, metrics, cache)\\n- ✅ Need lifecycle hooks for observability\\n- ✅ Testing isolation (enable/disable toggle)\\n- ✅ Decorator-based handler registration\\n\\n**When NOT needed**:\\n\\n- ❌ Simple event persistence (use direct &#96;outbox_repo.add_event()&#96;)\\n- ❌ Single handler per event\\n- ❌ No need for hooks/tracing\\n\\n📚 **[EventBus Documentation](./docs/eventbus/usage-guide.md)** - Complete guide for advanced patterns\\n\\n## Distribution\\n\\n- PyPI distribution name: &#96;python-eventing&#96;\\n- Python import package: &#96;messaging&#96;\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\\n# Install\\npip install python-eventing\\n\\n# Import\\nfrom messaging.core import BaseEvent\\nfrom messaging.infrastructure import SqlAlchemyOutboxRepository\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\nServices should consume the published package rather than source checkout. Kafka remains shared infrastructure with local producer/consumer clients per service.\\n\\n## Local development\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;powershell\\npoetry install\\npoetry build\\npoetry run pytest\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n### Testing\\n\\n**Local (Windows):**\\n- Run non-RabbitMQ tests: &#96;poetry run pytest tests/ -v -m \\\"not requires_rabbitmq\\\"&#96;\\n- RabbitMQ integration tests fail due to Docker Desktop networking limitations\\n\\n**CI/CD (Recommended for complete coverage):**\\n- GitHub Actions runs all 192 tests on Linux (100% pass rate)\\n- Workflows configured in &#96;.github/workflows/&#96;\\n- See &#96;.github/workflows/README.md&#96; for details\\n\\n**For complete local test coverage on Windows:** See [Windows Testing with WSL2](docs/WINDOWS_TESTING_WITH_WSL2.md) (requires Docker Desktop WSL integration setup).\\n\\n\"}}"
}

    docs(readme): reorder sections for better flow
    
    ## Starting point
    
    The "When to use this package" section appeared immediately after
    "Installation", before the comparison table, which interrupted the
    natural flow from requirements to alternatives comparison.
    
    ## Changes
    
    Moved "When to use this package" section in README.md:
    - From: lines 46-60 (after Installation section)
    - To: lines 71-84 (after Comparison with alternatives table)
    
    Also cleaned up minor whitespace:
    - Removed trailing spaces in setup example code
    - Removed trailing newline at end of file
    
    ## Result
    
    Documentation now flows more logically:
    1. Installation
    2. Comparison table (detailed feature breakdown)
    3. When to use (guidance based on that comparison)
    4. Scope
    
    The detailed comparison table now precedes the decision guidance,
    allowing readers to understand capabilities before making choices.
    
    Moved using safe-line-mover MCP for deterministic copy/paste.

```

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-max-thinking)**_

Yes, there are a few potential issues with that section:

1. **Format context missing**: The TOML snippet shows a raw dependency line without explaining where it goes. It should clarify this goes in your project's `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.poetry.dependencies]`.

   No version specified. The integration guide shows `python-eventing = "^0.1.0"` with a pinned version.

@@ -7,19 +7,29 @@ in-process dispatch and reliable cross-service publication.
 
 ## Install the package
 
-Use a normal package dependency instead of a source checkout:
+Add to your project's &#96;pyproject.toml&#96;:
 
 &#96;&#96;&#96;toml
+[tool.poetry.dependencies]
+python = "^3.12"
 python-eventing = "^0.1.0"
 &#96;&#96;&#96;
 
+Or install directly:
+
+&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+pip install python-eventing
+# or
+poetry add python-eventing
+&#96;&#96;&#96;
+
 The published distribution name is &#96;python-eventing&#96;, while Python imports use
 &#96;from messaging ...&#96; (not &#96;eventing&#96;).
 
 ## Add a new event schema
 
 1. Create a Pydantic event model that extends
-   &#96;eventing.core.contracts.BaseEvent&#96;.
+   &#96;messaging.core.BaseEvent&#96;.
 2. Declare a stable default &#96;event_type&#96; so the registry can auto-register it.
 3. Keep payload fields transport-safe and JSON-serializable.
 
@@ -32,8 +42,8 @@ producer payload.
 
 1. Register the event class in an &#96;EventRegistry&#96;.
 2. Add dispatcher registrations through
-   &#96;eventing.core.contracts.build_dispatcher&#96; or the higher-level
-   &#96;eventing.core.contracts.build_event_bus&#96;.
+   &#96;messaging.core.build_dispatcher&#96; or the higher-level
+   &#96;messaging.core.build_event_bus&#96;.
 3. Include the outbox writer handler so in-process dispatch automatically
    persists the event inside the same application transaction.
 
@@ -44,16 +54,18 @@ hooks while keeping the current outbox and Kafka data plane unchanged.
 ## Publish through the outbox
 
 1. Persist the domain event with
-   &#96;eventing.infrastructure.outbox.OutboxEventHandler&#96;.
-2. Let &#96;eventing.infrastructure.outbox.OutboxWorker&#96; read unpublished rows.
-3. Publish via &#96;eventing.infrastructure.messaging.KafkaEventPublisher&#96;.
-4. Route terminal failures through
-   &#96;eventing.infrastructure.messaging.DeadLetterHandler&#96;.
+   &#96;messaging.infrastructure.OutboxEventHandler&#96;.
+2. Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC automatically monitors the outbox table and
+   publishes to Kafka (no manual polling needed).
+3. Configure CDC with the Outbox Event Router SMT to route events to topics
+   based on event type.
+
+See {doc}&#96;debezium-cdc-architecture&#96; for CDC connector configuration details.
 
 ## Consume idempotently
 
 Create inbound consumers by extending
-&#96;eventing.infrastructure.messaging.IdempotentConsumerBase&#96;. This keeps

 cross-service handlers small and ensures replay-safe event handling when Kafka
 redelivers a message.

Let me provide a clear explanation of what CDC is, particularly in the context of their eventing system.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-max-thinking)**_

**CDC = Change Data Capture**

It's a pattern for tracking changes in a database and streaming those changes to other systems in real-time.

## How it works in your eventing system:

1. **Database writes happen**: Your application writes events to the `outbox_events` table in PostgreSQL as part of the same transaction as your business data

2. **CDC monitors the database**: Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC plugin watches the PostgreSQL Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for any changes to the `outbox_events` table

3. **Changes are captured**: When a new row is inserted into `outbox_events`, Debezium detects it immediately (within milliseconds)

4. **Events are published**: Debezium automatically publishes the captured change to Kafka topics

## Key benefits:

- **No polling**: Unlike reading the outbox table in a loop, CDC reacts to changes in real-time
- **Reliable**: Uses PostgreSQL's WAL (Write-Ahead Log), which is designed for durability
- **At-least-once delivery**: If publishing fails, CDC retries automatically
- **Transactional guarantee**: The outbox write and business data write are in the same DB transaction

## In your architecture:

```
Your Service → PostgreSQL outbox_events table
                    ↓ (Debezium CDC monitors WAL)
              Kafka Connect
                    ↓
              Kafka Topics
                    ↓
         Consumer Services
```

This is why the integration guide mentions "no manual polling needed" - CDC handles all the publishing automatically by watching the database.

commit 40a8651328047676e10fc539281625fbc84087bd
Author:     FirstUnicorn &lt;fl1xdf8u@anonaddy.me&gt;
AuthorDate: Sun Apr 19 01:32:07 2026 +0300
Commit:     FirstUnicorn &lt;fl1xdf8u@anonaddy.me&gt;
CommitDate: Sun Apr 19 01:45:25 2026 +0300

    docs(integration-guide): fix unclear installation and incorrect CDC description
    
    ## Starting point
    
    Integration guide had two critical issues preventing successful onboarding:
    
    1. **Unclear installation**: Showed bare dependency line &#96;python-eventing = "^0.1.0"&#96;
       without explaining where to place it or providing alternative install methods.
    
    2. **Incorrect CDC publishing description**: Referenced non-existent classes
       (OutboxWorker, KafkaEventPublisher, DeadLetterHandler) instead of accurately
       describing how Kafka Connect with Debezium CDC handles publishing.
    
    ## Changes
    
    Updated docs/source/integration-guide.md:
    
    ### Installation section (lines 8-27)
    - Added complete &#96;[tool.poetry.dependencies]&#96; context showing where to place
      the dependency line
    - Added alternative &#96;pip install&#96; and &#96;poetry add&#96; commands matching README
    - Clarified the distinction between distribution name (&#96;python-eventing&#96;)
      and import package (&#96;messaging&#96;)
    
    ### Publish through outbox section (lines 54-63)
    - Removed references to non-existent OutboxWorker, KafkaEventPublisher,
      DeadLetterHandler classes
    - Replaced with accurate CDC-based architecture: Kafka Connect with Debezium
      automatically monitors outbox table via PostgreSQL WAL and publishes to Kafka
    - Added cross-reference to debezium-cdc-architecture documentation
    
    ### Secondary fixes
    - Corrected outdated &#96;eventing.*&#96; import paths to &#96;messaging.*&#96; throughout
      (lines 32, 45-46, 57, 68)
    
    ## Result
    
    Integration guide now provides complete installation context and accurately
    describes the CDC-based outbox publishing architecture used in production.
    Users can successfully install the package and understand how events flow
    from outbox table to Kafka without manual polling.

```
